News App documentation¶

Add your content using reStructuredText syntax. See the reStructuredText documentation for details.

Database models for the News App.

Defines the core data structures used in the application, including users, publishers, articles, and newsletters.

class news.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)¶

Represents a news article written by a journalist.

Articles may belong to a publisher and must be approved by an editor before becoming visible to readers.

exception DoesNotExist¶
exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶
exception NotUpdated¶
class news.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)¶

Custom user model extending Django’s AbstractUser.

Users can have different roles such as reader, editor, or journalist. Readers can subscribe to publishers or individual journalists to receive article updates.

exception DoesNotExist¶
exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶
exception NotUpdated¶
class news.models.Newsletter(*args, **kwargs)¶

Represents a newsletter containing a collection of articles.

Newsletters are created by a user and can include multiple articles.

exception DoesNotExist¶
exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶
exception NotUpdated¶
class news.models.Publisher(*args, **kwargs)¶

Represents a news publisher.

Articles can optionally be associated with a publisher, and users can subscribe to publishers to receive their articles.

exception DoesNotExist¶
exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶
exception NotUpdated¶

Main web views for the News App.

This module handles user authentication, dashboards, article creation, editing, approvals, subscriptions, and article browsing for the web interface of the application.

news.views.approve_articles(request)¶

Allow editors to review pending articles.

Editors can approve or decline submitted articles. Approved articles trigger email notifications to subscribers.

news.views.article_list_view(request)¶

Display a list of all approved articles.

news.views.create_article(request)¶

Allow journalists to create new articles.

Articles are created with approved=False and must be approved by an editor before publication.

news.views.dashboard(request)¶

Display the user dashboard.

The dashboard content may vary depending on the role of the logged-in user.

news.views.edit_article(request, article_id)¶

Allow the author of an article to edit its title and content.

news.views.home(request)¶

Render the home page of the News App.

news.views.login_view(request)¶

Authenticate a user and log them into the system.

If credentials are invalid, an error message is shown.

news.views.logout_view(request)¶

Log the user out and redirect to the home page.

news.views.signup(request)¶

Handle user registration.

Displays a registration form and creates a new user when valid data is submitted.

news.views.subscribed_articles_view(request)¶

Display articles from journalists and publishers that the user is subscribed to.

news.views.toggle_subscribe_journalist(request, journalist_id)¶

Subscribe or unsubscribe the current user from a specific journalist.

API views for the News App.

This module provides REST API endpoints for interacting with articles. It allows authenticated users to retrieve articles, create new articles, update their own articles, and view articles from subscribed journalists or publishers.

class news.api_views.ArticleDetailUpdateDeleteAPI(**kwargs)¶

API endpoint to retrieve, update, or delete a specific article.

Access is restricted to authenticated users who are either the article’s author or an editor.

perform_update(serializer)¶

Update the article and send a notification if the article has just been approved.

serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class news.api_views.ArticleListCreateAPI(**kwargs)¶

API endpoint for listing approved articles and creating new ones.

GET:

Returns all approved articles.

POST:

Allows authenticated journalists to create a new article. Articles are created with approved=False and must be approved by an editor before becoming publicly visible.

get_permissions()¶

Apply different permissions depending on request method.

POST requests require the user to be an authenticated journalist. GET requests require the user to be authenticated.

get_queryset()¶

Return the queryset based on request type.

GET requests return only approved articles. Other request types return all articles.

perform_create(serializer)¶

Save the article with the current user as the author and mark it as not approved.

serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class news.api_views.ArticleSerializer(*args, **kwargs)¶

Serializer for the Article model.

Converts Article instances into JSON format and ensures that certain fields (author and approved status) are read-only.

class news.api_views.PublisherSerializer(*args, **kwargs)¶

Serializer for the Publisher model.

class news.api_views.SubscribedArticlesAPI(**kwargs)¶

API endpoint that returns approved articles from journalists or publishers that the authenticated user is subscribed to.

get(request)¶

Retrieve approved articles written by journalists or publishers that the current user follows.

news.api_views.approved_article(request, *args, **kwargs)¶

API endpoint used to log or acknowledge when an article has been approved by an editor.

Serializers for the News App.

This module provides serializers for converting Django models into JSON for the REST API, and for validating incoming data when creating or updating objects via the API.

class news.serializers.ArticleSerializer(*args, **kwargs)¶

Serializer for the Article model.

Includes nested publisher information and read-only author username. Used for listing, retrieving, creating, and updating articles via the API.

class news.serializers.NewsletterSerializer(*args, **kwargs)¶

Serializer for the Newsletter model.

Serializes newsletter details including related articles and author information.

class news.serializers.PublisherSerializer(*args, **kwargs)¶

Serializer for the Publisher model.

Converts Publisher instances to JSON and validates incoming data for creating or updating publishers.

class news.serializers.UserSerializer(*args, **kwargs)¶

Serializer for the CustomUser model.

Includes basic user information and lists of subscribed publishers and journalists.

News App
